mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real stock prices rise while the federal funds rate falls simultaneously, household wealth effects amplify consumption demand and lower financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform independent single-series signals, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.3% reflects robust manufacturing and capacity utilization that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate sustained labor market strength and rising aggregate wages, which directly boost corporate profit expectations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns in >60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 450,000 units signal a sharp contraction in residential construction and related consumer durables demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered months, or the threshold shows no predictive edge over a simple linear trend model.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and rising unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or 20-day returns are positive in >65% of stagflationary observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict returns during financial crisis periods, or 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with GDP growth in >55% of quarters.
